title: emplace should be prefered over insert with std::set and std::unordered_set
description: emplace enables you to avoid copying or moving the value you are about to insert and, instead, it constructs it in-place with the arguments provided. Prefer using emplace, or emplace_hint if all the conditions hold: You are inserting a single value. You are constructing a fresh temporary value just to insert it into the set.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Use emplace() instead of insert() with temporary objects for better performance
